laws.title: Florida Minimum Wage ‑ ‐#15.00/hr Coming September 30, 2026
laws.summary: Florida's minimum wage is on a path to $15.00/hr by September 30, 2026, as mandated by Amendment 2 (voter-approved in 2020). The current rate is $14.00/hr (effective Sept 30, 2025). On September 30, 2026, the rate increases to $15.00/hr — the final scheduled step. Tipped employees: current direct wage is $10.98/hr; will increase to $11.98/hr on Sep  30, 2026 (with a $3.02 tip credit). After 2026, future increases will be CPI-indexed annually.
compliance_actions: Plan budget now for the $1.00/hr increase on September 30, 2026
Update tipped employee direct wage to $11.98/hr by September 30, 2026
Post the updated Florida Minimum Wage notice by September 30, 2026
Update employment agreements, offer letters, and pay structures before the effective date
Audit all hourly roles to confirm compliance by the September 30 deadline

laws.title: Florida E-Verify Requirement — Mandatory for All Employers (July 2023)
laws.summary: Florida SB 1718 (effective July 1, 2023) requires all private employers to use the federal E-Verify system to confirm work authorization of new hires. This applies to all employers — not just those with government contracts. Penalties for non-compliance include suspension of business licenses for repeat violations. Employers must retain E-Verify records for at least 3 years.
compliance_actions: Ensure all new hires in Florida are processed through E-Verify (not just I-9)
Retain E-Verify records for a minimum of 3 years
Train HR/hiring managers on the mandatory E-Verify process
Do not terminate or refuse to hire based on preliminary non-confirmation — follow the process
